comparison.description: Large adult-body Co-Cr rod — mandatory 510(k) anchor tier

algorithms.claim: MAR implementation 'Spectral MAR' demonstrates NON-INFERIOR lesion detectability vs noMAR for T1_AB (Large adult-body Co-Cr rod — mandatory 510(k) anchor tier; 41% angular blockage, CoCr) with ΔAUC = -0.0119 (95% CI [-0.0363, +0.0112]; one-tailed Wilcoxon p = 0.0000).

MAR implementation 'iMAR' demonstrates NON-INFERIOR lesion detectability vs noMAR for T1_AB (Large adult-body Co-Cr rod — mandatory 510(k) anchor tier; 41% angular blockage, CoCr) with ΔAUC = -0.0144 (95% CI [-0.0381, +0.0037]; one-tailed Wilcoxon p = 0.0000).

comparison.description: Small pediatric-body SS-316L rod — supplemental tier

algorithms.claim: MAR implementation 'ASD-iMAR' demonstrates INDETERMINATE lesion detectability vs noMAR for T2_SB (Small pediatric-body SS-316L rod — supplemental tier; 40% angular blockage, SS316L) with ΔAUC = -0.0659 (95% CI [-0.1137, -0.0359]; one-tailed Wilcoxon p = 0.0886).

MAR implementation 'DLSC-iMAR' demonstrates INDETERMINATE lesion detectability vs noMAR for T2_SB (Small pediatric-body SS-316L rod — supplemental tier; 40% angular blockage, SS316L) with ΔAUC = -0.0544 (95% CI [-0.1219, -0.0062]; one-tailed Wilcoxon p = 0.5920).

MAR implementation 'FS-iMAR' demonstrates INDETERMINATE lesion detectability vs noMAR for T2_SB (Small pediatric-body SS-316L rod — supplemental tier; 40% angular blockage, SS316L) with ΔAUC = -0.2619 (95% CI [-0.3331, -0.1925]; one-tailed Wilcoxon p = 0.0013).

MAR implementation 'MBIR-iMAR' demonstrates INDETERMINATE lesion detectability vs noMAR for T2_SB (Small pediatric-body SS-316L rod — supplemental tier; 40% angular blockage, SS316L) with ΔAUC = -0.0556 (95% CI [-0.0888, -0.0187]; one-tailed Wilcoxon p = 0.0000).

MAR implementation 'Spectral MAR' demonstrates SUPERIOR lesion detectability vs noMAR for T2_SB (Small pediatric-body SS-316L rod — supplemental tier; 40% angular blockage, SS316L) with ΔAUC = +0.0488 (95% CI [+0.0037, +0.0981]; one-tailed Wilcoxon p = 0.0000).

MAR implementation 'iMAR' demonstrates INDETERMINATE lesion detectability vs noMAR for T2_SB (Small pediatric-body SS-316L rod — supplemental tier; 40% angular blockage, SS316L) with ΔAUC = -0.2625 (95% CI [-0.3381, -0.1913]; one-tailed Wilcoxon p = 0.0001).

comparison.description: Circular head phantom Ti-6Al-4V rod — supplemental tier

algorithms.claim: MAR implementation 'FS-iMAR' demonstrates NON-INFERIOR lesion detectability vs noMAR for T3_HEAD (Circular head phantom Ti-6Al-4V rod — supplemental tier; 16% angular blockage, Ti6Al4V) with ΔAUC = +0.0194 (95% CI [-0.0238, +0.0581]; one-tailed Wilcoxon p = 0.0014).

MAR implementation 'POCS-TV' demonstrates INDETERMINATE lesion detectability vs noMAR for T3_HEAD (Circular head phantom Ti-6Al-4V rod — supplemental tier; 16% angular blockage, Ti6Al4V) with ΔAUC = -0.0194 (95% CI [-0.0550, +0.0062]; one-tailed Wilcoxon p = 0.0155).

MAR implementation 'Spectral MAR' demonstrates INDETERMINATE lesion detectability vs noMAR for T3_HEAD (Circular head phantom Ti-6Al-4V rod — supplemental tier; 16% angular blockage, Ti6Al4V) with ΔAUC = -0.0281 (95% CI [-0.0644, -0.0025]; one-tailed Wilcoxon p = 0.0010).

MAR implementation 'iMAR' demonstrates NON-INFERIOR lesion detectability vs noMAR for T3_HEAD (Circular head phantom Ti-6Al-4V rod — supplemental tier; 16% angular blockage, Ti6Al4V) with ΔAUC = -0.0069 (95% CI [-0.0475, +0.0275]; one-tailed Wilcoxon p = 0.0063).

traceability.hold_out: Leave-One-Out (LOO) with observer re-fit per fold
traceability.wilcoxon: One-tailed paired Wilcoxon on d[i] = s_LP[i] − s_LA[i]
